info.title: Vestream Vesting Data API
info.description:
Normalised, chain-indexed token vesting data — the same dataset that powers the Vestream dashboard and the @vestream/mcp MCP server, exposed as a plain REST API for agents, integrations, and back-office tooling.

## What this API is for
Every vesting protocol exposes its own subgraph, contract layout, and data shape. Integrating even one of them takes weeks; integrating eight across two ecosystems takes a team. This API hides that complexity behind a single cross-protocol, cross-chain, cross-ecosystem normalised schema — so an agent or app can answer questions like "what unlocks for this wallet in the next 14 days" or "what is this stream's claimable balance right now" in a single call, regardless of whether the wallet is an EVM 0x… address or a Solana base58 pubkey.

## Coverage
- **Protocols:** Sablier, Hedgey, UNCX (TokenVesting + VestingManager), Unvest, Team Finance, Superfluid, PinkSale (PinkLock V2), Streamflow (Solana)
- **EVM chains:** Ethereum (1), BNB Chain (56), Polygon (137), Base (8453)
- **Non-EVM chains:** Solana (101)
- **Address formats:** EVM 0x-prefixed hex OR Solana base58 pubkeys — the API auto-detects the ecosystem from the input
- **Data shape:** one `VestingStream` schema for every protocol on every chain — same fields, same units, same ID format

## Authentication
All endpoints require an API key passed as a bearer token:
```
Authorization: Bearer vstr_live_...
```
Keys are issued per account from `/developer/account` after access is approved. Free-tier keys are capped at 30 req/min burst and 150 req/day; paid-tier keys are scoped per contract.

## Data freshness
Responses are served from Vestream's persistent index — not live on-chain queries. The `last_indexed` field on responses tells you how fresh the data is; it typically trails chain tip by under a minute. No endpoint makes direct RPC or subgraph calls at request time.

## Rate limits
Every response carries `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers. A 429 means you've hit the monthly cap or the burst/daily window for your tier. Back off and retry — or contact us about a higher tier.

Request a key: [vestream.io/developer](https://vestream.io/developer)

BearerAuth.description: API key issued by Vestream. Format: `vstr_live_` followed by 64 hex characters. Generate keys from /developer/account once access is approved.

VestingStream.description: A single normalised vesting stream. This is the canonical shape every protocol is mapped to — Sablier's per-second linear streams, UNCX's milestone locks, Hedgey's NFT plans, and PinkSale's cycle-based unlocks all come back in this format. Amounts are raw integer strings (not floats — use `tokenDecimals` to format for display). Timestamps are unix seconds.

id.description: Composite ID in the format `{protocol}-{chainId}-{nativeId}`. EVM examples: `sablier-1-12345`, `uncx-8453-99`. Solana example: `streamflow-101-7xKXtg2CW87d97TXJSDpbD5jBkheTqA83TZRuJosgAsU`. Use with GET /stream/{streamId} to fetch full detail. URL-safe.

protocol.description: Source protocol. `uncx-vm` is UNCX's VestingManager contract — merged with `uncx` in most product surfaces. `streamflow` and `jupiter-lock` are Solana-only; all others are EVM.

chainId.description: Network identifier. EVM chains: 1 (Ethereum), 56 (BNB Chain), 137 (Polygon), 8453 (Base). Non-EVM: 101 (Solana).

recipient.description: Wallet that receives the vesting tokens. EVM addresses are lowercased hex; Solana pubkeys are base58 (case-sensitive — preserved as returned).

tokenAddress.description: Contract address of the token. EVM: ERC-20 contract (lowercase hex). Solana: SPL mint pubkey (base58).

tokenSymbol.description: Human-readable symbol as reported by the token contract. May be empty if the contract doesn't implement the symbol() view.

tokenDecimals.description: ERC-20 decimals. Divide raw amounts by 10^decimals to get a display amount.

totalAmount.description: Total tokens allocated to this stream, as a raw integer string (e.g. `"1000000000000000000000"` for 1000 tokens at 18 decimals). String format because bigint values exceed JavaScript's safe integer range.

withdrawnAmount.description: Amount already claimed by the recipient to date (raw integer string).

claimableNow.description: Amount vested and immediately claimable at the time of the last index. `totalAmount - withdrawnAmount - lockedAmount` gives you this value.

lockedAmount.description: Amount still locked — not yet vested. Goes to 0 when the stream is fully vested.

startTime.description: Stream start (unix seconds). For cliff schedules, vesting doesn't actually begin until `cliffTime`.

endTime.description: Stream end (unix seconds). At this point `lockedAmount` becomes 0 and `isFullyVested` becomes true.

cliffTime.description: Unix timestamp at which the cliff unlocks (null if the schedule has no cliff). Between `startTime` and `cliffTime`, no tokens are claimable.

isFullyVested.description: True when `lockedAmount === 0`. `claimableNow` may still be > 0 if the recipient hasn't withdrawn the final tranche yet.

nextUnlockTime.description: Unix timestamp of the next incremental unlock — useful for building alerts. Null when the stream is fully vested or streams continuously (linear schedules return the end time here).

cancelable.description: Whether the stream can be cancelled by the sender. Protocol-specific; null when the protocol doesn't expose this.

shape.description: Stream shape. `linear` means continuous per-second vesting (Sablier, Superfluid). `steps` means discrete unlocks at fixed times (UNCX, Team Finance, PinkSale tranched schedules).

unlockSteps.description: Full ordered list of discrete unlock events for step-shaped schedules — each element has a `timestamp` (unix seconds) and `amount` (raw integer string). Empty/null for linear schedules.

claimEvents.description: Historical claim / withdrawal transactions, newest first. Useful for computing time-series of claim behaviour. Each event has `timestamp` (unix seconds) and `amount` (raw integer string of tokens claimed).

Error.description: Standard error envelope returned for any non-2xx response.

error.description: Short machine-readable error slug suitable for branching in client code.

docs.description: Link to the relevant section of these docs.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Monthly request quota for your API key.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining requests in the current month.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp at which the monthly counter resets.

get.summary: List all vesting streams for a wallet
get.description:
Returns every indexed vesting stream where the given wallet is the recipient — across all 7 supported protocols and all 4 chains in a single call. Use this to build portfolio views, power wallet-level dashboards, or answer questions like "how much of token X is this wallet vesting across Sablier and Hedgey combined?"

Responses include linear and step-shaped streams, claimed and unclaimed balances, cliff times, and the full `unlockSteps` array for stepped schedules — everything a client needs to render a timeline or compute a forecast without a second call.

Cache hint: data is served from Vestream's index, not from live on-chain queries. The `last_indexed` timestamp tells you how fresh it is; this call does not trigger a fresh subgraph fetch.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Wallet address. EVM: 0x-prefixed hex, case-insensitive (the API lowercases before lookup). Solana: base58-encoded pubkey, case-sensitive. The API auto-detects ecosystem from the input format.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of protocol IDs to restrict the scan to. Omit for all protocols. Valid values: `sablier`, `uncx`, `hedgey`, `unvest`, `team-finance`, `superfluid`, `pinksale`, `streamflow`, `jupiter-lock`.

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of chain IDs. Omit for all supported chains. Valid values: `1` (Ethereum), `56` (BNB Chain), `137` (Polygon), `8453` (Base), `101` (Solana).

parameters.name: active_only
parameters.description: When true, excludes streams where `isFullyVested === true`. Useful for building live dashboards where you only care about streams still releasing tokens. Default: false (returns all streams including historical/finished ones).

200.description: List of vesting streams matching the filters. An empty `streams` array means the wallet has no indexed vestings on the selected protocols/chains — not an error.

wallet.description: The wallet address that was queried, lowercased.

count.description: Number of streams in the response.

last_indexed.description: When the backing index was last refreshed for this wallet's protocols. Typically under one minute old.

401.description: Missing, malformed, or revoked API key.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded — either the per-minute burst, per-day, or monthly quota for your tier.

get.summary: List upcoming unlock events for a wallet
get.description:
Returns every unlock event (cliff completion, tranche release, linear stream endpoint) scheduled to occur within a future time window, sorted ascending by date. Purpose-built for forecasting, alerts, and claim-planning agents: one call tells you exactly what tokens a wallet can claim over the next N days and when.

Typical uses: agents building unlock calendars, bots that notify users before an unlock, compliance tools tracking vesting cash-flow exposure. Prefer this endpoint over `GET /wallet/{address}/vestings` when you only care about the next unlock per stream — it's ~10× cheaper to parse on the client.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Wallet address — EVM 0x hex (case-insensitive) or Solana base58 pubkey (case-sensitive).

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Lookahead window in days, counting from now. Default: 30. Maximum: 365 (values above are clamped).

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description: Comma-separated protocol filter — same semantics as on the vestings endpoint. Omit to include every protocol.

200.description: Upcoming unlock events sorted ascending by `unlock_time`. An empty `unlocks` array means nothing vests for this wallet within the window.

unlocks.description: Each element is one scheduled unlock event. Multiple events may belong to the same `stream_id` when a schedule has multiple tranches inside the window.

stream_id.description: Composite stream ID — pass to GET /stream/{streamId} for full detail.

unlock_time.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when this event fires.

unlock_type.description: `cliff` = a cliff completion; `tranche` = a step in a stepped schedule; `end` = a linear stream's fully-vested timestamp.

amount_unlocking.description: Tokens releasing at this event (raw integer string; divide by token decimals for display).

401.description: Missing, malformed, or revoked API key.

get.summary: Get full detail for a single vesting stream
get.description:
Returns every field of the `VestingStream` schema for one specific stream, plus the indexer-level metadata (`first_seen`, `last_indexed`). Use this after discovering a `stream_id` from the wallet-level endpoints — never as a first call unless you already have the composite ID from elsewhere (an on-chain event, a user-pasted URL, etc.).

Stream IDs are composite: `{protocol}-{chainId}-{nativeId}`. Example: `sablier-1-12345` is Sablier stream 12345 on Ethereum. IDs are stable and URL-safe.

parameters.name: streamId
parameters.description: Composite stream ID in the format `{protocol}-{chainId}-{nativeId}`, always lowercase. Obtainable via the wallet endpoints or directly constructible if you have the protocol and native ID.

200.description: Full stream detail, including `first_seen` (when the indexer first saw this stream) and `last_indexed` (how fresh the data is).

401.description: Missing, malformed, or revoked API key.

404.description: Stream ID not found in the Vestream index. Possible causes: the ID is malformed, the protocol isn't indexed, or the stream was created after the last subgraph sync (rare — usually resolves within a minute).

tags.name: Wallet
tags.description: Wallet-level queries — use these when your input is an address and you want everything indexed for it.

tags.name: Streams
tags.description: Stream-level queries — use these when you already have a specific stream ID in hand.
